session_summary.label: session_summary
session_summary.summary:
## Goal
Continue the auth refresh race fix. Implement/validate an async queue-based refresh coordinator that deduplicates refresh work per tenant without over-serializing unrelated tenants.

## Instructions
- Prioritize auth work over docs cleanup.
- Do not revive the old docs/markdown parser caching hypothesis; it was wrong and unrelated noise for the auth fix.
- Avoid returning to the failed mutex design except as prior evidence.
- Keep the queue helper API narrow, centered on `enqueueRefresh`.
- Ensure same-tenant refresh work is coalesced and different tenants can proceed independently.
- Register in-flight work synchronously before any `await`.

## Discoveries
- `MutexRefreshCoordinator` failed under the concurrent test `auth_refresh_deduplicates_parallel_requests`.
- A mutex can fail if scoped per handler/request/instance instead of shared per tenant.
- Final direction is an async queue/coalescing helper.
- Rollback should be controlled by `FLAG_AUTH_QUEUE_ROLLBACK`.
- Needed tests:
  - same-tenant refresh dedupe — most important
  - different-tenant parallelism
  - rollback flag behavior
  - failure propagation when queued refresh rejects
- On rejection, all coalesced callers should receive the failure, and a later retry should be able to enqueue fresh work.

## Accomplished
- Decided against the mutex-oriented approach.
- Chosen final design direction: async per-tenant queue/coalescing.
- Identified rollback flag and helper location.
- Separated docs cleanup from auth work.

## Relevant files / directories
- `src/auth/queue.ts`
- `FLAG_AUTH_QUEUE_ROLLBACK`
- Test name to preserve: `auth_refresh_deduplicates_parallel_requests`
